creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
After some usage of mail server running on this FreeBSD 8.1 machine I
am not able to create any files there (no error returned), its
portfix/dovecot/spamassassin working there with MAILDIR files for each
account (to be precise one heavy used account).

# mount
/dev/label/root on / (ufs, local)
/dev/label/var on /var (ufs, local)
/dev/label/mail on /usr/mail (ufs, local, soft-updates)
/dev/md0 on /tmp (ufs, asynchronous, local)

All mail account are kept on separate filesystem like that:
  /usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new

When I am at that pwd(1)
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new

I am not able to create any files after about 4-5 hours of heavy usage,
its like that:


/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # :> asd
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # echo $?
0
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # ls asd
ls: asd: No such file or directory
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # echo $?
1

If I reboot, everything goes back to normal and I can create files there:
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # :> asd
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # echo $?
0
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # ls asd
asd
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN/new # echo $?
0

I do not think its about lack of free inodes, I created that filesystem
(/dev/label/mail) with these parameters:
# newfs -U -i 65536 -h 8192 -m 1 /dev/label/mail

# df -i
Filesystem      1K-blocks    Used   Avail Capacity iused ifree %iused  Mounted on
/dev/label/root    380716  323020   53890    86%   19940 29210   41%   /
devfs                   1       1       0   100%       0     0  100%   /dev
/dev/label/var      63252    7166   55454    11%     756  7434    9%   /var
/dev/label/mail   4175514 1880612 2253148    45%   20604 46274   31%   /usr/mail
/dev/md0            47342      10   47332     0%       5  6393    0%   /tmp

# df -m
Filesystem      1M-blocks Used Avail Capacity  Mounted on
/dev/label/root       371  315    52    86%    /
devfs                   0    0     0   100%    /dev
/dev/label/var         61    6    54    11%    /var
/dev/label/mail      4077 1836  2200    45%    /usr/mail
/dev/md0               46    0    46     0%    /tmp

Here are number of files/dirs there:

/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN # find . -type f | wc -l
   18085
/usr/mail/DOMAIN/USER@DOMAIN # find . -type d | wc -l
      93


There are no error messages on postfix/dovecot/spamassassin daemon logs,
nothing in dmesg, anyway, here are outputs of sysctl -a | dmesg |
/etc/sysctl.conf (I have added these but they did not helped) and
/boot.loader.conf 

/boot/loader.conf 
  autoboot_delay=1

/etc/sysctl.conf 
  kern.coredump=0
  kern.maxfiles=1239232
  kern.maxfilesperproc=1239232
  kern.maxprocperuid=1222222
  kern.sigqueue.max_pending_per_proc=2000
  kern.threads.max_threads_per_proc=15900
